decision_points.required_input: The name and kind (SecretStore or ClusterSecretStore) of the external secret store to reference in all ExternalSecret CRDs. Example: 'openclaw-aws-secrets' (ClusterSecretStore) or 'vault-main' (SecretStore).
decision_points.impact: This determines the .spec.secretStoreRef.name and .spec.secretStoreRef.kind fields in all ExternalSecret manifests. If not provided, manifests will be incomplete and cannot be applied.

decision_points.required_input: Choose one: (1) Bare Deployment/StatefulSet (no operator dependency), (2) Spotahome redis-operator CR, (3) Bitnami Helm chart. If using an operator or Helm, specify if the operator/chart is already installed in the cluster.
decision_points.impact: This determines whether the Redis manifest is a simple Deployment+Service (option 1), a Redis CR (option 2), or a HelmRelease/Helm chart (option 3). If operator/chart is not present, additional installation steps are required.

blocking_questions: What is the name and kind (SecretStore or ClusterSecretStore) of the external secret store to use for all ExternalSecret CRDs in sigma-1-dev?
Should Redis be deployed as a bare Deployment+Service (no operator), as a Spotahome redis-operator CR, or via Bitnami Helm chart? If operator/chart, is it already installed?

next_steps.details: Provide the external secret store name/kind and select the Redis deployment mechanism.

next_steps.step: Once resolved, generate manifests for all 12 files in the scaffold, using the provided field structure and subtask details.

reason: Provisioning is blocked until the external secret store reference and Redis deployment method are specified. All other requirements are clear and can be implemented once these decisions are made.

scaffold_dependencies.reason: PM server requires Linear API credentials and infra endpoints as environment variables, which are provided by these manifests. The deployment manifest for the PM server must mount these as envFrom sources.

missing_k8s_resources.name: pm-server
missing_k8s_resources.reason: No deployment manifest for the PM server is present in the provided scaffold. This is required to mount the ConfigMap and Secret as environment variables.

scaffold_modifications_required.description: Must contain all required infra endpoint environment variables for the PM server, including Linear team ID and any other relevant endpoints.
Must create a Secret with the Linear API token, named so it can be referenced as envFrom in the PM server deployment.
Create a Deployment manifest for the PM server, mounting the above ConfigMap and Secret via envFrom. Set container envFrom for both resources.

test_stub_commands: kubectl get configmap sigma-1-infra-endpoints -n sigma-1-dev -o yaml
kubectl get secret sigma-1-linear-api-token -n sigma-1-dev -o yaml
kubectl exec -it <pm-server-pod> -n sigma-1-dev -- env | grep LINEAR

function_signatures.name: computeIdempotencyKey
function_signatures.signature: computeIdempotencyKey(prdContent: string, taskId: string | number): string

function_signatures.name: findExistingIssue
function_signatures.signature: findExistingIssue(linearClient, teamId: string, idempotencyKey: string): Promise<string | null>

function_signatures.name: withRetry
function_signatures.signature: withRetry<T>(fn: () => Promise<T>, options: { maxRetries: number, baseDelayMs: number, stage: string }): Promise<T>

function_signatures.name: pipelineLog
function_signatures.signature: pipelineLog(entry: { level: 'debug'|'info'|'warn'|'error', stage: string, [key: string]: unknown }): void

decision_points_analysis.recommended_option: JSON/YAML config file mounted via ConfigMap
decision_points_analysis.reason: Allows hot-reload of delegate mappings without redeploying the PM server, is simple to implement, and avoids DB dependency. Can be mounted as a file or env var via ConfigMap.

decision_points_analysis.recommended_option: Embed a deterministic key (PRD hash + task index) in the issue description body and search via Linear's issue filter API
decision_points_analysis.reason: Does not require additional DB or local state, is robust to restarts, and leverages Linear's search/filter capabilities. Using an HTML comment ensures the key is not visible to end users.

unit_tests: resolve_agent_delegates returns correct mapping and undefined for unknown agents
computeIdempotencyKey returns deterministic keys for same inputs
integration_tests: Issue creation flow creates issues with correct assigneeId and skips duplicates
POST /api/pipeline/delegate-status returns correct mapping and metadata
idempotency_tests: Second run of the same PRD creates zero new issues, all are skipped
error_handling_tests: Invalid delegate results in unassigned issue and error log
Linear API retry logic retries on failure and logs attempts
logging_tests: All logs are valid JSON and include required fields

implementation_plan.description: Update or create PM server deployment manifest to mount sigma-1-infra-endpoints ConfigMap and sigma-1-linear-api-token Secret via envFrom.
Implement resolve_agent_delegates and getDelegateMap in src/delegates/resolve-agent-delegates.ts, loading mapping from env or config file.
Implement idempotency logic in src/pipeline/idempotency.ts and integrate into issue creation flow.
Wire delegate resolution into issue creation, passing assigneeId if resolved, logging errors if not.
Wrap Linear API calls with withRetry for exponential backoff and logging.
Add POST /api/pipeline/delegate-status endpoint in Elysia, returning current delegate mapping.
Standardize structured JSON logging across all pipeline stages using pipelineLog.

task_analysis.title: Integrate Hermes Research in Deliberation Path (Nova - Bun/Elysia)
task_analysis.summary: Integrate Hermes SaaS research API into the PM server's deliberation pipeline (Bun/Elysia stack), with a circuit breaker, timeout, fallback, and health check. Ensure NOUS_API_KEY gating, structured logging, and persistence of research memos for downstream validation.
key_requirements: Hermes API client module with NOUS_API_KEY authentication and error handling.
Circuit breaker (timeout, failure threshold, reset) around Hermes calls; Bun-compatible.
Availability gating: skip Hermes if NOUS_API_KEY is missing.
Fallback memo generation for circuit open, timeout, or Hermes errors.
Integrate Hermes research into deliberation pipeline after PRD parsing, before task generation.
Persist research memo in deliberation artifacts for downstream use.
Expose Hermes circuit breaker state in GET /api/pipeline/status.
Comprehensive test suite for all scenarios (success, timeout, circuit open, no API key, etc.).
decision_points: Confirm circuit breaker library compatibility with Bun (opossum vs. cockatiel vs. custom).
Resolve Hermes SaaS API contract: endpoint, request/response schema, auth header.
Decide on research memo persistence: DB vs. in-memory pipeline state.

subtasks.title: Implement Hermes API client module with NOUS_API_KEY authentication
Implement circuit breaker wrapper with configurable timeout, failure threshold, and reset
Implement availability gating for missing NOUS_API_KEY
Implement fallback behavior for circuit-open, timeout, and error scenarios
Integrate Hermes research call into the deliberation pipeline stage
Add circuit breaker state to GET /api/pipeline/status health check
Write comprehensive test suite for the Hermes integration

test_strategy: Unit test: mock Hermes API returning valid research content; assert the deliberation output contains Hermes-sourced content with source: 'hermes'.
Timeout test: mock Hermes API with 35s delay; assert circuit breaker opens after timeout and fallback memo is generated within 31s.
Circuit breaker test: trigger 3 consecutive failures; assert subsequent calls return fallback immediately without HTTP calls for 60s, then half-open allows one probe.
No-API-key test: unset NOUS_API_KEY; assert deliberation completes with skip log entry and no HTTP calls to Hermes.
Integration test: run deliberation with a real or stubbed Hermes endpoint; assert the returned research memo has non-empty content field and is persisted in the deliberation artifacts.
acceptance_criteria: Hermes research is invoked in the deliberation pipeline only if NOUS_API_KEY is set.
Circuit breaker prevents blocking the event loop on Hermes API latency or repeated failures.
Fallback memo is generated and logged on Hermes unavailability, circuit open, or timeout.
Research memo (Hermes, fallback, or skip) is persisted in deliberation artifacts.
GET /api/pipeline/status returns Hermes circuit breaker state and API key configured status.
Test suite covers all major scenarios and edge cases.

components.name: DesignSnapshotPRList
components.description: Displays a list/table of design snapshot PRs with status, repo, branch, date, and GitHub link. Handles loading, empty, and error states. Consumes useDesignSnapshotPRs hook.

components.name: DesignSnapshotPRDetail
components.description: Displays full metadata for a selected PR and lists all scaffold files. Handles loading, empty, and error states. Consumes useDesignSnapshotPRDetail hook.

hooks.name: useDesignSnapshotPRs
hooks.description: Fetches PR list data from GET /api/pipeline/design-prs scoped by pipelineRunId. Returns { data, isLoading, error, retry }.

hooks.name: useDesignSnapshotPRDetail
hooks.description: Fetches scaffold file listing for a single PR by its ID. Returns { data, isLoading, error, retry }.

pages.name: Design PRs Dashboard Page
pages.description: New dashboard route for design snapshot PRs. Accepts pipelineRunId as query param. Hosts the list and detail components.

navigation.name: Sidebar/Tab Entry
navigation.description: Adds a 'Design PRs' link/tab to the dashboard navigation, matching existing style and auth guards.

types.name: DesignSnapshotPR
types.description: Defines PR metadata: id, title, status, repoName, branchName, createdAt, githubUrl, scaffoldFiles?

types.name: ScaffoldFile
types.description: Defines scaffold file: filename, path, status

accessibility.name: Accessibility Enhancements
accessibility.description: Add aria-labels, semantic HTML, keyboard navigation, and focus management. Ensure axe-core compliance.

test_stubs.name: Component Render Tests
test_stubs.description: Test loading, empty, error, and populated states. Assert on DOM elements, links, and accessibility.

test_stubs.name: Hook Unit Tests
test_stubs.description: Test hook loading, success, error, and retry logic with fetch mocks.

test_stubs.name: Accessibility Tests
test_stubs.description: Run axe-core on all component states to ensure zero violations.

decision_points: Route vs Tab: Default to new route /dashboard/design-prs unless app's dashboard is strictly tab-based.
API contract: Assume above response shape; if actual API differs, update interfaces and data mapping accordingly.
Detail view: Use drill-down route /dashboard/design-prs/[prId] if app supports dynamic routes, else expandable panel in list.
Data fetching: Use existing project pattern (SWR, React Query, or fetch/useEffect) — do not introduce new libraries.
UI: Use only existing design system components for cards, tables, badges, loading, and icons.
test_strategy: Component renders with valid PR data (mock 2+ PRs).
Empty state renders when API returns empty array.
Error state renders with retry button on fetch failure.
Each PR card shows title, status badge, repo, branch, date, and GitHub link.
Detail view lists at least one scaffold file with filename and path.
Accessibility: axe-core scan returns zero violations.
Navigation entry is present and routes correctly.
Tab/keyboard navigation covers all interactive elements.

files.description: Notification dispatcher module with uniform interface, dependency-injectable HTTP client, and graceful degradation. Orchestrates Discord and Linear bridge notifications with retry and logging.
Discord bridge notification sender. Formats payload, sets Authorization header, POSTs to Discord bridge URL, throws on error, logs on success.
Linear bridge notification sender. Formats payload (including session ID and delegate assignments), sets Authorization header, POSTs to Linear bridge URL, throws on error, logs on success.
Generic retry utility with exponential backoff, structured logging, and injectable sleep function. Used by dispatcher to wrap bridge notification calls.

integration_points.description: Pipeline orchestrator entry point. Instantiates notification dispatcher with config from environment/ConfigMap. Calls notify('pipeline_start', ...) at start and notify('pipeline_complete', ...) at end (in finally block), with structured logging at each hook.

targets: dispatcher: calls both notifiers, handles failures gracefully, logs warnings
discord-notifier: correct POST, headers, payload, error on 4xx/5xx
linear-notifier: correct POST, headers, payload, error on 4xx/5xx
retry: retries with exponential backoff, logs attempts, throws after max
pipeline orchestrator: notifications fire at start/end, pipeline continues on notification failure, logs present

task_analysis.title: Validate End-to-End Pipeline Completion (Tess - Test frameworks)
task_analysis.summary: This task requires building and executing a comprehensive E2E test suite to validate the Sigma-1 intake pipeline, ensuring it completes all stages (deliberation, task generation, issue creation, agent assignment, notifications) for a single PRD. The test suite must use the existing test framework (Jest, Vitest, etc.), run against the deployed dev environment, and assert all acceptance criteria via 7 test cases. The suite must be CI-ready, outputting a JUnit XML report.
acceptance_criteria: Pipeline returns status: 'complete' with a valid pipelineRunId.
Task count >= 5 with all required fields populated.
>= 80% of tasks have non-null delegate_id or assigneeId.
PR URL matches GitHub sigma-1 repo pattern.
env_vars_required: PM_AUTH_TOKEN (or equivalent, depending on auth scheme)

steps: Check package.json for jest, vitest, or other test runner dependencies.
Look for jest.config.*, vitest.config.*, or test config in package.json.
Identify test directory convention (e2e/, tests/, __tests__/, etc.).

test_cases.title: Full pipeline execution
test_cases.action: POST sample PRD to PM server's pipeline trigger endpoint, assert 200 response, pipelineRunId present, status: 'complete'.

test_cases.title: Task generation
test_cases.action: GET /api/pipeline/runs/{runId}/tasks, assert >=5 tasks, each with non-empty title, agent, stack.

test_cases.title: Agent assignments
test_cases.action: Assert >=80% of tasks have non-null delegate_id or assigneeId.

test_cases.title: PR creation
test_cases.action: Assert prUrl is non-null and matches https://github.com/5dlabs/sigma-1/pull/\d+.

test_cases.title: No fatal errors
test_cases.action: Assert PM server logs for the run contain zero entries with level: 'fatal' or level: 'error' and fatal: true.

decision_points.topic: Pipeline trigger endpoint and auth mechanism
decision_points.recommendation: Inspect PM server route definitions to confirm endpoint and auth scheme; parameterize via env vars.

decision_points.topic: Log inspection mechanism for fatal error check
decision_points.recommendation: Prefer a dedicated API endpoint on the PM server for log queries; fallback to embedded logs/errors in pipeline run object if not available.

subtasks.title: Detect existing test framework and configure E2E test scaffold
Implement pipeline trigger test and timing SLA assertion
Implement task generation and agent assignment validation tests
Implement Linear session and PR creation validation tests
Implement no-fatal-errors log validation test
Execute full E2E test suite and generate JUnit XML report

task_analysis.test_strategy: All 7 test cases must pass in a single run against the deployed dev environment. JUnit XML report must be generated and contain exactly 7 test case entries. Failures must be categorized and documented.

files.description: End-to-end test suite for verifying Linear issue delegate assignments, agent mapping, and metadata. Uses Linear GraphQL API directly.
Fixture mapping agent hints to expected Linear user IDs for delegate assignment validation.

tests.name: Setup: Load pipeline issue IDs, delegate mapping, and fetch Linear issue data
assertions: At least 5 issue IDs are loaded from pipeline results
Delegate mapping contains at least 4 entries
GraphQL responses for all issues are valid (no error fields)

tests.name: Assignee presence: >= 80% of issues have a non-null assignee
assertions: Count of issues with assignee !== null >= Math.max(4, Math.ceil(totalIssues * 0.8))

tests.name: Correct mapping: Each assigned issue's assignee.id matches expected Linear user ID
assertions: For each assigned issue, assignee.id === expectedUserId from delegate mapping (skip if no mapping for agent hint)

tests.name: Unassigned handling: Unassigned issues correspond to unmapped agent hints and have PM server error logs
assertions: For each unassigned issue, agent hint is not present in delegate mapping
PM server error log entry exists for each unassigned issue mentioning unmapped agent hint

tests.name: No agent:pending labels: No issues have placeholder assignment labels
assertions: No issue has a label matching /^agent[:\-_]?pending$/i or similar

tests.name: Issue metadata: All issues have non-empty title, description, and correct project/team
assertions: Each issue.title and issue.description is a non-empty string
Each issue.project.id === expectedProjectId
Each issue.team.id === expectedTeamId

external_dependencies: Lightweight GraphQL client (e.g., graphql-request or fetch)
Access to pipeline run results (issue IDs and agent hints) from Task 6 output or endpoint
Access to PM server logs (file or endpoint) for error validation
notes: All Linear API queries must use the LINEAR_API_TOKEN from environment
No PM server API calls for issue verification (only for error log lookup)
Test suite is parallelizable except for setup and mapping tests

scaffold_analysis.reasoning: This task and its subtasks are focused on implementing and validating end-to-end tests for Hermes research memo integration in the PM server pipeline. The work is entirely within the test codebase (e2e/hermes-research-validation.test.ts) and does not require any changes to Kubernetes manifests, infrastructure, or deployment configuration. The only relevant environment variable is NOUS_API_KEY, which is assumed to be set/unset in the test environment, not managed via new or changed ExternalSecret or ConfigMap resources. No new services, CRDs, or infra resources are needed for this testing work.

e2e/design-pr-surfacing.test.ts.description: End-to-end Playwright test suite for Design Snapshot PR surfacing in the dashboard. Validates section visibility, PR data rendering, GitHub link correctness, scaffold file listing, empty state, accessibility, and pipeline scoping.

test_cases.title: Design Snapshot PR section is visible and accessible
steps: Launch browser and authenticate if required.
Navigate to /dashboard.
Locate the Design Snapshot PR section/tab using getByRole or locator (labelled 'Design Snapshot' or similar).
Assert the section/tab is visible.
If tabbed, click to activate and assert the content panel appears.
Tab to the section element and assert it receives keyboard focus.
assertions: Section/tab is present in the DOM.
Section/tab is visible.
Section/tab is keyboard focusable.

test_cases.title: PR card displays correct metadata after pipeline run
steps: Navigate to the Design Snapshot PR section.
Locate at least one PR card (e.g., [data-testid='pr-card']).
Within the card, find elements for title, status badge, repo name, and date.
assertions: Title is a non-empty string.
Status badge text matches /open|merged|closed/i.
Repo name contains '5dlabs/sigma-1'.
Date matches /\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}/.

test_cases.title: PR card contains valid GitHub link
steps: Within a PR card, locate the anchor/link element.
Check href, target, and rel attributes.
assertions: href matches https://github.com/5dlabs/sigma-1/pull/\d+.
target equals '_blank'.
rel contains 'noopener'.

test_cases.title: PR detail view lists scaffold files
steps: Click PR card or expand detail button.
Wait for detail view to appear.
Locate scaffold file list items.
assertions: At least one scaffold file is listed.
Each file has a non-empty filename.

test_cases.title: Empty state message is shown when no PRs exist
steps: Mock API to return empty PR list using page.route.
Navigate to Design Snapshot PR section.
Locate empty state element.
assertions: Empty state element is visible.
Text matches /No design snapshot PRs found/i.

test_cases.title: Accessibility: zero critical/serious axe-core violations
steps: Navigate to Design Snapshot PR section with data present.
Run axe-core accessibility scan on the section.
assertions: No violations with impact 'critical' or 'serious'.

test_cases.title: PRs are scoped to the current pipelineRunId
steps: Navigate to dashboard with ?pipelineRunId=run-abc-123.
Collect all PR cards and extract pipelineRunId metadata.
Assert all PRs match run-abc-123.
Navigate with a different pipelineRunId (e.g., run-xyz-456).
Assert previous PRs are not shown.
assertions: All displayed PRs have pipelineRunId matching the query param.
No cross-run PR data is visible.

plan.summary: Production hardening for the Sigma-1 dev Kubernetes infrastructure, focusing on HA, ingress/TLS, network security, RBAC, secret rotation, audit logging, and documentation. Each subtask is mapped to a concrete YAML or Helm change, with testable outcomes and documentation requirements.

decision_points.name: Redis HA strategy
decision_points.decision: Use Bitnami Redis Helm chart with Sentinel enabled (sentinel.enabled=true) for robust HA, as the operator in use does not natively support Sentinel/replicated mode.

decision_points.name: Ingress controller selection
decision_points.decision: Use Nginx Ingress Controller, as it is the most widely supported and compatible with cert-manager and required annotations.

decision_points.name: TLS certificate provisioning
decision_points.decision: Use cert-manager with Let's Encrypt (ClusterIssuer) for automated certificate management and renewal.

decision_points.name: External secrets backing store
decision_points.decision: Assume AWS Secrets Manager as the backend, as it is commonly used and supports rotation policies.

decision_points.name: Audit logging approach
decision_points.decision: Use a Falco DaemonSet in the namespace to capture secret access and security events, as cluster-level audit policy changes may not be permitted.

file_changes.description: Set spec.instances: 3, ensure failover settings are present, and pg_hba allows replication.
Bitnami Redis Helm values YAML for 3-node Redis with Sentinel enabled.
Update REDIS_URL to use Sentinel-aware connection string.
Ingress resource for PM server with TLS, rate limiting, and body size annotations.
ClusterIssuer for cert-manager using Let's Encrypt.
Add default deny-all policy and explicit allowlist policies for required ports and FQDNs.
Remove any wildcard or cluster-admin bindings. Add Role/RoleBinding for PM server with least privilege.
Dedicated Roles/RoleBindings for all service accounts in the namespace.
Add spec.refreshInterval: 1h to all ExternalSecret CRs.
Helm values or manifest for stakater/reloader to trigger rolling restarts on secret changes.
Falco DaemonSet manifest for audit logging in sigma-1-dev namespace.
Comprehensive documentation of all hardening measures, rationale, configuration, and verification.

subtasks.title: Scale CloudNative-PG cluster to 3 replicas with automated failover
implementation: Edit k8s/sigma-1-dev/cnpg-cluster.yaml: set spec.instances: 3, ensure failoverDelay is set (e.g., 10s), and pg_hba allows replication.
Apply: kubectl apply -f k8s/sigma-1-dev/cnpg-cluster.yaml -n sigma-1-dev
Test: kubectl get cluster sigma-1-pg -n sigma-1-dev -o jsonpath='{.status.readyInstances}' == 3; delete primary pod and verify failover.

subtasks.title: Configure Redis HA with Sentinel and update ConfigMap connection string
implementation: Create k8s/sigma-1-dev/redis-ha.yaml: Bitnami Redis Helm values for 3 Redis nodes and 3 Sentinels.
Deploy: helm upgrade --install sigma-1-redis bitnami/redis -f k8s/sigma-1-dev/redis-ha.yaml -n sigma-1-dev
Update k8s/sigma-1-dev/configmap-infra-endpoints.yaml: set REDIS_URL to redis-sentinel://sentinel-0:26379,sentinel-1:26379,sentinel-2:26379/sigma-1-redis
Test: redis-cli -h <sentinel-service> -p 26379 sentinel master sigma-1-redis

subtasks.title: Configure Ingress with TLS termination via cert-manager for PM server
implementation: Create k8s/sigma-1-dev/cert-issuer.yaml: ClusterIssuer for Let's Encrypt.
Create k8s/sigma-1-dev/pm-ingress.yaml: Ingress resource with TLS, rate limiting, and body size annotations.
Apply: kubectl apply -f k8s/sigma-1-dev/cert-issuer.yaml; kubectl apply -f k8s/sigma-1-dev/pm-ingress.yaml
Test: kubectl get certificate sigma-1-pm-tls -n sigma-1-dev; curl -k https://<ingress-host>/api/pipeline/status

subtasks.title: Harden Cilium NetworkPolicies with deny-all default and exact port allowlisting
implementation: Update k8s/sigma-1-dev/cilium-networkpolicies.yaml: add default deny-all policy and explicit allowlist for required ports/services/FQDNs.
Apply: kubectl apply -f k8s/sigma-1-dev/cilium-networkpolicies.yaml
Test: Deploy nettest pod and verify denied/allowed connections as per test strategy.

subtasks.title: Audit and tighten RBAC with dedicated Roles for all service accounts
implementation: Audit: kubectl get rolebindings,clusterrolebindings -n sigma-1-dev -o yaml
Update k8s/sigma-1-dev/pm-serviceaccount.yaml: remove wildcards, add least-privilege Role/RoleBinding for PM server.
Create k8s/sigma-1-dev/roles.yaml: Roles/RoleBindings for all SAs.
Apply: kubectl apply -f k8s/sigma-1-dev/pm-serviceaccount.yaml; kubectl apply -f k8s/sigma-1-dev/roles.yaml
Test: kubectl auth can-i --as=system:serviceaccount:sigma-1-dev:sigma-1-pm-sa create pods -n sigma-1-dev

subtasks.title: Configure secret rotation via external-secrets with refreshInterval and rolling restart triggers
implementation: Update all k8s/sigma-1-dev/externalsecret-*.yaml: add spec.refreshInterval: 1h
Create k8s/sigma-1-dev/reloader.yaml: stakater/reloader manifest or Helm values.
Annotate Deployments with reloader.stakater.com/auto: "true"
Apply: kubectl apply -f k8s/sigma-1-dev/externalsecret-*.yaml; helm install reloader stakater/reloader -n sigma-1-dev
Test: Modify secret in AWS Secrets Manager, wait for refresh, verify resourceVersion and pod restart.

subtasks.title: Add audit logging for namespace security events
implementation: Create k8s/sigma-1-dev/falco.yaml: Falco DaemonSet manifest for sigma-1-dev.
Apply: kubectl apply -f k8s/sigma-1-dev/falco.yaml
Test: Access a secret and verify Falco logs the event.

subtasks.title: Document all production hardening changes in docs/production-hardening.md
implementation: Create docs/production-hardening.md with sections for each hardening measure, rationale, YAML/config references, and verification commands.
Add summary table and ensure at least 6 sections and 500+ words.
Test: grep for section headers, check word count, and verify presence of rationale and verification in each section.

test_strategy: CloudNative-PG: status.readyInstances == 3; simulate failover by deleting primary pod.
Redis: redis-cli sentinel master returns valid master with 2 replicas; test failover.
Ingress: curl and openssl s_client to verify TLS and HTTP 200.
NetworkPolicies: nettest pod cannot connect to PM server; PM server can reach allowed services.
RBAC: kubectl auth can-i for each SA and verb.
Secret rotation: resourceVersion changes after backend update; pods restart automatically.
Audit logging: Falco logs secret access events.
Documentation: docs/production-hardening.md exists, 6+ sections, rationale and verification in each.
